name: org.machanism.machai:genai-client
description: GenAI Client (org.machanism.machai:genai-client) is a Java 8+ library that provides a provider-agnostic abstraction for integrating Generative AI capabilities into JVM applications within the MachAI ecosystem. The library centers around the org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai interface and the org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager factory, enabling applications to select and configure a concrete GenAI backend using a single provider spec string in the form Provider:Model (for example, "OpenAI:gpt-4.1" or "CodeMie:gpt-4o-mini"). It supports common LLM workflows: composing prompts (org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai#prompt(java.lang.String)), applying system instructions (org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai#instructions(java.lang.String)), executing a request to obtain a response (org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai#perform()), and collecting per-call token usage metrics (org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai#usage(), org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Usage) aggregated via org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager. The library also exposes an extensible host-side tool/function calling framework: tool installers implement org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools and are discovered using java.util.ServiceLoader by org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader, then registered into a Genai instance via org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai#addTool(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ToolFunction, java.lang.String...). Built-in tools include safe(ish) command execution and workflow termination (org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.CommandFunctionTools registers "run_command_line_tool" and "terminate_process") plus web retrieval and generic REST calls (org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.WebFunctionTools registers "get_web_content" and "call_rest_api"). Command execution is constrained by deny lists (src/main/resources/denylist/windows.txt and unix.txt) and by project-root working directory confinement. Provider implementations included are: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.openai.OpenAIProvider (OpenAI Java SDK Responses API for prompting, iterative tool-call resolution, and embeddings via com.openai.models.embeddings), org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.codemie.CodeMieProvider (EPAM CodeMie token acquisition with delegation to OpenAI/Gemini/Claude based on chatModel prefix), org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.none.NoneProvider (no-op provider that can log prompts locally), plus placeholders for org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.gemini.GeminiProvider and org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.claude.ClaudeProvider (not fully implemented). Configuration is supplied via org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator and/or environment variables. Key configuration/environment keys include: chatModel (selected model name), OPENAI_API_KEY and optional OPENAI_BASE_URL for OpenAI-compatible backends, GENAI_TIMEOUT / MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS / MAX_TOOL_CALLS for OpenAI request tuning, embedding.model for embedding generation, and GENAI_USERNAME / GENAI_PASSWORD / AUTH_URL for CodeMie token retrieval. The library is intended to be embedded as a dependency by other MachAI modules or any Java application that needs a unified API for LLM prompting, tool calling (agent-like workflows), and optional embeddings for tasks such as semantic search, content generation, code assistance, and automated project assembly.

constructors.description: Resolves and instantiates a concrete org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai implementation from a provider/model identifier formatted as Provider:Model (e.g., "OpenAI:gpt-4.1"). If the provider portion is omitted, the default provider is org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.none.NoneProvider. The method sets conf["chatModel"] to the parsed model name and calls org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai#init(org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator). Providers are instantiated via reflection either by short provider name mapping to org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.<providerLowercase>.<Provider>Provider or by fully-qualified class name.

examples.description: Creates an OpenAI-backed Genai instance using org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.openai.OpenAIProvider and applies configuration from the configurator, including an embedding model for org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai#embedding(java.lang.String,long).

Creates a CodeMie-backed Genai instance using org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.codemie.CodeMieProvider. The provider retrieves an OAuth token from CodeMie and configures an OpenAI-compatible downstream provider automatically.

If the Provider: prefix is omitted, GenaiProviderManager defaults to org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.none.NoneProvider and treats the full string as the model portion. This is useful for disabling AI while keeping the same call sites in code.

features.name: prompt
features.description: Adds a user prompt to the current provider session. Providers typically accumulate prompts (and optional attachments/tools) until org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai#perform() is invoked.

examples.description: Builds a request by adding instructions and a prompt, then executes the provider to obtain a response string.

features.name: instructions
features.description: Sets system-level instructions for the current conversation/session (provider-dependent). In org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.openai.OpenAIProvider, this maps to the OpenAI Responses API instructions field.

examples.description: Uses instructions to influence response style and constraints without modifying the user prompt content.

features.name: perform
features.description: Executes the provider based on accumulated inputs and returns the response text. In OpenAIProvider, perform() also supports iterative function tool call resolution: if the model requests a tool, the provider invokes the registered org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ToolFunction and sends the tool output back to the model until a final message is produced.

examples.description: Sends the request to the provider and returns the model response text.

features.name: embedding
features.description: Computes an embedding vector for the given text (provider-dependent). org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.openai.OpenAIProvider uses com.openai.models.embeddings.EmbeddingCreateParams and requires the configuration key embedding.model to be set to a valid embedding model id.

examples.description: Generates an embedding suitable for semantic search indexing. The dimensions value is provider/model specific and may be ignored or rejected depending on the selected embedding model.

features.name: addTool
features.signature: void org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai.addTool(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String description, org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ToolFunction function, java.lang.String... paramsDesc)
features.description: Registers a tool (function) that the provider may invoke during execution. In OpenAIProvider, paramsDesc entries use the format name:type:required:description and are translated into a JSON Schema parameters object. Tool handlers receive provider-specific arguments; OpenAIProvider passes a com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode for arguments and a java.io.File working directory.

examples.description: Registers a simple tool callable by the model and then prompts the model to call it. The tool contract matches what OpenAIProvider expects (JsonNode args + working dir).

features.name: applyTools
features.description: Discovers and applies org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools implementations via java.util.ServiceLoader. In this artifact, META-INF/services registers org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.WebFunctionTools and org.machanism.machai.gw.tools.CommandFunctionTools, which install HTTP/REST and command execution tools.

examples.description: Loads built-in host tools (web fetch, REST, command execution, termination) and registers them into the provider so the model can request these actions during tool calling.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai
customizations.description: Primary extension point for adding new provider integrations. Implementations should support init(Configurator), prompt/instructions accumulation, perform() execution, optional embedding generation, and optional tool registration semantics.

examples.description: Sketch of a custom provider implementing the Genai interface. A fully-qualified provider class name can be passed to GenaiProviderManager.getProvider(...) to instantiate it via reflection.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
customizations.description: SPI for installing host-provided function tools into a Genai instance. Implementations are typically discovered using java.util.ServiceLoader and can resolve ${...} placeholders via an injected org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator.

examples.description: Defines a tool installer and registers a simple tool. To make it discoverable, add a line with the fully qualified class name to META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools in your jar.

customizations.name: run_command_line_tool denylist
customizations.description: Command execution is constrained by deny lists loaded from classpath resources (denylist/windows.txt and denylist/unix.txt) and by project-root working directory confinement (CommandFunctionTools#resolveWorkingDir). Host applications can further restrict exposure of these tools by not registering CommandFunctionTools.

examples.description: Demonstrates how the host can customize tool availability by controlling which FunctionTools are present/applied.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai
studs.description: Provider contract that calling code depends on. Implementations adapt external GenAI backends (OpenAI, CodeMie, etc.) to a uniform API for prompting, execution, tool registration, embeddings, and usage reporting.

examples.description: Uses the Genai interface directly so that switching providers requires only a configuration change (Provider:Model spec).

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
studs.description: SPI implemented by host tool installers to register one or more callable tools into a Genai instance. Discovered via java.util.ServiceLoader and applied by FunctionToolsLoader.

examples.description: Applies all discovered FunctionTools implementations to a provider, enabling tool calling workflows.

examples.title: Install as a Maven dependency
steps: Add the dependency to your pom.xml.
Ensure you are using Java 8+.
Provide required environment variables (for example OPENAI_API_KEY) or set them via Configurator.
examples.notes: For OpenAIProvider you must set OPENAI_API_KEY. For CodeMieProvider set GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD.

examples.title: Basic prompting with OpenAI provider
steps: Create a Configurator and set OPENAI_API_KEY.
Resolve a provider using GenaiProviderManager.getProvider("OpenAI:gpt-4.1", conf).
Add instructions and prompts.
Call perform() and read the response.
Optionally read usage() and aggregate via GenaiProviderManager.logUsage().
examples.notes: OpenAIProvider uses the OpenAI Java SDK Responses API and can iteratively resolve tool calls if tools are registered.

examples.title: Enable tool calling: register built-in web + command tools via ServiceLoader
steps: Resolve an OpenAI provider.
Set a working directory using genai.setWorkingDir(new File(".")).
Create FunctionToolsLoader and call setConfiguration(conf) so header placeholders can be resolved.
Call loader.applyTools(genai) to register get_web_content, call_rest_api, run_command_line_tool, and terminate_process.
Prompt the model to use a tool (provider-dependent).
Call perform(); the provider will execute requested tools and send outputs back to the model until it returns a final message.
examples.notes: The get_web_content tool supports textOnly and selector; run_command_line_tool should be invoked with cmd /c on Windows.

examples.title: CLI-like scenario: run a safe command tool on Windows
steps: Resolve an OpenAI provider and apply CommandFunctionTools (via FunctionToolsLoader).
Set working directory to the project root.
Ask the model to execute a Windows command using cmd /c (required for proper shell execution).
The command output is bounded by tailResultSize and checked against deny lists.
The model uses the tool output to produce a final answer.
examples.notes: The tool call payload should use {"command":"cmd /c dir","dir":"."}. The command is checked against deny lists and cannot escape the project directory.

examples.title: CodeMie provider: delegated OpenAI-compatible usage
steps: Set GENAI_USERNAME and GENAI_PASSWORD (or set them on Configurator).
Resolve provider using CodeMie:<model>.
Execute prompts as usual; CodeMieProvider will acquire an OAuth token and delegate to the downstream provider based on model prefix.
examples.notes: CodeMieProvider sets OPENAI_BASE_URL to https://codemie.lab.epam.com/code-assistant-api/v1 and injects the retrieved access token as OPENAI_API_KEY before delegating.
